description: Defines virtual fields for a material type. Virtual fields exist only in the query API surface — they expand into real monk3 QueryNode templates when queried. Document block names (e.g. 'root', 'chapter') must match blocks declared in the corresponding mapping.json file.

$schema.description: Optional JSON Schema URI for editor validation of virtual mapping documents.

^(?!\\$schema$).*$.description: A document block (e.g. 'root' or a named subdocument type). Each property defines one virtual field.

virtualField.description: A virtual field that does not exist in the search backend. When queried, its 'expansion' QueryNode template is substituted with values from the incoming QueryPayload (or, for 'predicate' fields, expanded statically) and translated instead.

type.description: Accepted data type for this virtual field, determining what the API consumer may pass when querying it. The 'string'/'freetext'/'number'/'datetime'/'boolean' types each accept a single leaf QueryPayload. The special 'predicate' type accepts NO data — it is queried by field name alone and expands statically. The 'subquery' type accepts a boolean query (an array of clause arrays, the BooleanQueryData structure) — typically substituted into an expansion that targets a subdocument field.

expansion.description: A monk3 QueryNode template (same structure as the 'query' field in SearchQueryRequest). String values may contain '{{data}}' as a whole-string placeholder, replaced at query time with the entire incoming QueryPayload JSON object. Only whole-string placeholders are supported (e.g. "{{data}}", not "prefix-{{data}}"). For 'predicate' fields the expansion is fully static and must NOT contain '{{data}}'. Expansion templates may only reference real mapped fields — not other virtual fields.
